THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE SOUTH END OF STOCKING LAKE AND TRACKED NORTH NORTHEAST FOR NEARLY 14 MILES. IT CROSSED INTO HUBBARD COUNTY ABOUT 4 NNE OF MENAGHA BY 920 AM CDT. IT THEN TRACKED AN ADDITIONAL 9 MILES AND LIFTED ABOUT 3NE OF PARK RAPIDS BY 934 AM CDT. MAXIMUM TORNADO WIDTH WAS ABOUT 450 YARDS WITH PEAK WINDS TO 130 MPH. EIGHT LARGE TURKEY BARNS WERE DESTROYED AND SEVERAL HOMES DAMAGED ALONG WITH HUNDREDS OF ACRES OF FOREST.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE SOUTH END OF STOCKING LAKE AND CONTINUED FOR NEARLY 14 MILES TO THE NORTH NORTHEAST...CROSSING OVER HINDS LAKE...AND LIFTING ABOUT 3NE OF PARK RAPIDS BY 934 AM CDT. MAXIMUM TORNADO WIDTH WAS ABOUT 450 YARDS WITH PEAK WINDS TO 130 MPH. EIGHT LARGE TURKEY BARNS WERE DESTROYED ALONG WITH ONE HOUSE AND HUNDREDS OF ACRES OF FOREST. SEVERAL HOMES AND BUSINESSES FROM STOCKING LAKE TO PARK RAPIDS ALSO RECEIVED DAMAGE.

THIS TORNADO ORIGINATED IN WADENA COUNTY. IT CONTINUED NORTH NORTHEAST WHERE IT CROSSED HINDS LAKES AND PASSED ALONG THE EAST EDGE OF PARK RAPIDS. IT LIFTED ABOUT 3NE OF PARK RAPIDS BY 934 AM CDT. MAXIMUM TORNADO WIDTH WAS ABOUT 450 YARDS WITH PEAK WINDS TO 130 MPH. THE TORNADO DESTROYED ONE HOME AND HUNDREDS OF ACRES OF FOREST. SEVERAL HOMES AND BUSINESS FROM HINDS LAKE TO PARK RAPIDS RECEIVED DAMAGE.
REPORT FROM PUBLIC RELAYED TO LAW ENFORCEMENT.

TORNADO PRODUCED WIDESPREAD ROOF DAMAGE EAST SIDE OF PARK RAPIDS.TREES DOWN CARS IN DITCH 1 MILE NORTH OF HWY 34 ON CTY ROAD 4 NEAR EMMAVILLE/LAKE GEORGE.TREES DOWN SOUTH OF TOWN. TORNADO MOVING TO THE NORTHEAST.

THIS TORNADO TRACKED FOR ABOUT 7 MILES FROM THE SOUTHWEST EDGE OF BIG SAND LAKE...ACROSS PICKEREL LAKE...TO 1NW OF EMMAVILLE BY 947 AM CDT. MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS AROUND 400 YARDS WITH PEAK WINDS TO 160 MPH. TORNADO COMPLETELY DESTROYED TWO HOMES AND DAMAGED SEVERAL OTHERS ON PICKEREL LAKE. IT FLATTENED DOZENS OF ACRES OF FOREST.

TORNADO TRACKED FOR ABOUT 7 MILES FROM THE SOUTHWEST EDGE OF BIG SAND LAKE...ACROSS PICKEREL LAKE...TO 1NW OF EMMAVILLE BY 947 AM CDT. MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS AROUND 400 YARDS WITH PEAK WINDS TO 160 MPH. TORNADO COMPLETELY DESTROYED TWO HOMES AND DAMAGED SEVERAL OTHERS ON PICKEREL LAKE. IT FLATTENED DOZENS OF ACRES OF FOREST.

THIS TORNADO RESULTED FROM A STORM SPLIT NEAR BIG SAND LAKE AND TRACKED TO THE NORTHEAST FOR NEARLY 5 MILES. IT HAD SEVERAL BRIEF TOUCHDOWNS BEFORE IT LIFTED ABOUT 5 ESE OF EMMAVILLE AND EAST OF MANTRAP LAKE BY 951 AM CDT. NO SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS REPORTED OR DETECTED BY AERIAL SURVEY THOUGH SOME TREE BRANCH DAMAGE WAS NOTED. ESTMATED MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 75 YARD WITH PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 80 MPH.
ONE HALF MILE WIDE PATH OF DESTRUCTION REPORTED FROM PARK RAPIDS NORTHWARD.
SEVERAL HOUSES DESTROYED BY TORNADO.

A TORNADO TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY FOR NEARLY 8 MILES TO AROUND 6 W LAKE GEORGE BY 9 PM CDT. TORNADO TRACKED FOR ABOUT 2 MILES TO AROUND 2 NORTH OF FOXHOME BY 6 PM CDT. A MACHINE SHED WAS MOVED OF ITS FOUNDATION. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE BROKEN DOWN OR SNAPPED OFF NEAR THE GROUND IN SHELTER BELTS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 95 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 50 YARDS.

SEVERAL FIELDS AND FARM ACCESS ROADS WERE FLOODED DUE TO RUNOFF FROM HEAVY RAINS IN NORTHEAST RICHLAND AND NORTHWEST WILKIN COUNTIES. RAINFALL RANGED FROM 3 TO 6 INCHES OVER SEVERAL TOWNSHIPS DURING A ROUGHLY 4 HOUR PERIOD ON THE EVENING OF THE 11TH. THIS FOLLOWED 1 TO 2 INCHES RAINFALL AMOUNTS OVER THE SAME AREA EARLIER IN THE DAY. WATER SLOWLY SUBSIDED THROUGH THE MORNING OF THE 12TH AS RUNOFF MOVED INTO THE LARGER RIVER SYSTEM.
